findings.evidence: Mathlib provides the natural API as `abbrev Nondegenerate (B : BilinForm R M) : Prop := LinearMap.Nondegenerate B` in `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/BilinearForm/Properties.lean:291`, and nearby TauCeti exposes analogous results as `(ω.symmetrizedBilinForm J).Nondegenerate` and `(ω.associatedBilinForm J).Nondegenerate` in `TameMetric.lean:125` and `CompatibleMetric.lean:153`.
findings.fix: Prove `theorem IsNegativeDefinite.intersectionForm_nondegenerate (h : P.IsNegativeDefinite) : P.intersectionForm.Nondegenerate` first, then derive the left-kernel lemma from `h.intersectionForm_nondegenerate.1` if it is still useful.
findings.issue: `IsNegativeDefinite.eq_zero_of_intersectionForm_left` is a specialized left-kernel consequence while the docstring and downstream use want nondegeneracy of `P.intersectionForm` itself.

summary: The sign statements are at the right level, but the advertised nondegeneracy result is exposed only as a bespoke left-kernel eliminator instead of the standard Mathlib bilinear-form API.
